summary_of_threads_started_this_week:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community have highlighted significant advancements and ongoing projects. The [btc-verified project](https://github.com/ProofOfKeags/btc-verified) has made notable progress using Lean4 for formal verification of Bitcoin's protocol, focusing on factual accuracy in design discussions and providing clear definitions and proofs of the system's operations. This method has underscored the non-malleability of the Merkle Root, establishing a dependable correlation with its tree, which is vital for the security and integrity of block transactions.

Simultaneously, the release of multiple Bitcoin Core versions for testing has been announced, with the latest being v31.1rc1, accessible for download and review from [Bitcoin Core's official page](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/test.rc1/). These release candidates, such as v30.3rc1 and v29.4rc1, serve as precursors to final versions, providing an opportunity for the community to identify and address any critical issues. Detailed information about these versions is available in the release notes on [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/v31.1rc1/doc/release-notes.md), encouraging community feedback to ensure stability and functionality before the official releases.

Moreover, the KVANTA5 blockchain has implemented a new signature scheme, ML-DSA-87, demonstrating robustness through extensive stress tests and transaction validations. This implementation highlights the chain's ability to handle large-scale operations and provides empirical data useful for ongoing cryptographic discussions. The technical details and transaction data are transparently shared for verification and research purposes through resources like the [KVANTA5 Explorer](https://kvanta5.live/) and [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Kvanta-Organization/Kvanta5-Core).

These developments reflect the Bitcoin community's commitment to enhancing protocol understanding, improving system reliability, and fostering an open testing environment to address any potential issues before official releases.

new_threads_this_week.title: btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
new_threads_this_week.summary: - Significant progress in Bitcoin's protocol verification using Lean4 is reported.  - Initial results highlight the non-malleability of the Merkle Root in Bitcoin's architecture.  - The project aims to refine understanding of Bitcoin's mechanics and encourage community feedback.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version v30.3rc1 is now ready for download and testing.
- Detailed release notes are available to help users understand version changes.
- The information was shared with the Bitcoin Development Mailing List subscribers.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1rc1 is now available
new_threads_this_week.summary: - Bitcoin Core version v31.1rc1 is now available for download and testing.  - Users are encouraged to report issues via the project's GitHub page.  - Feedback will determine the stability and release of the final version v31.1.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available for download as a minor version release.
- The release candidate source is hosted on GitHub for transparency and user contribution.
- Users can report issues with this test version directly on the project's GitHub tracker.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1rc1 is now available
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version v31.1rc1 is now available for preliminary testing.
- Users can download the test release and source code from the official Bitcoin Core website and GitHub.
- Feedback from testing is crucial for finalizing the stable release of this version.

new_threads_this_week.title: [Deleted discussion]
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- No specific email content was provided for summarization.
- Follow-up is required to obtain the key points or text from the email.
- Once provided, a structured and informative summary can be produced.

new_threads_this_week.title: Btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
new_threads_this_week.summary: - Formal Verification enhances software reliability, crucial for Bitcoin's protocol precision.  - The project uses Lean4 and mathlib to verify Bitcoin Improvement Proposals rigorously.  - Despite its advancements, the verification doesn’t encompass the entire Bitcoin Core behavior.

new_threads_this_week.title: Empirical ML-DSA-87 data from a live SHA-256 PoW chain (relevant to BIP-360 / Witness V3 sizing discussion)
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- ML-DSA-87 on KVANTA5 blockchain started June 1, 2026 without fallback mechanisms.
- A stress test analyzed transaction scalability and computational loads on the blockchain.
- All details on the implementation and tests are verifiable through the KVANTA5 blockchain explorer.

active_posts_this_week.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email discusses enhancing blockchain with merkle trees for better transaction validation.
- It proposes utilization of merkle trees to create cryptographic puzzles and compact proofs.
- The discussion includes considerations on blockchain's validation block costs and protocol improvements.

active_posts_this_week.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Antoine questions the security of disabling EC paths through a proposed tripwire mechanism.
- He argues that miners might not act against their economic interests, risking network security.
- Antoine doubts the proposal's game-theoretical robustness due to potential collusion risks.

active_posts_this_week.title: [BIP Proposal] Strict circular economy within the Bitcoin network
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Expiring UTXOs may be removed if not used within a set timeframe.
- State rent suggests higher fees for transactions with older UTXOs.
- Different UTXO policies affect the economic impact on miners and blockchain operations.

active_posts_this_week.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Entry content in blockchain is non-essential for consensus and can be discarded.  - Entry presence is critical only within a specific retention window for block validity.  - SegData allows for selective pruning within blocks, enhancing operational flexibility.

active_posts_this_week.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- PathCoin remains a theoretical concept unrelated to the main discussion.
- Initially meant as research foundation, it lacks practical application.
- Emphasizes the need for advancing theoretical ideas into practical solutions.

active_posts_this_week.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Various cryptocurrency transaction schemes are analyzed under specific conditions.  - P2TRv2 is vulnerable under standard conditions without disabled ECC.  - P2QR offers robust protection except when an ECC spend occurs.

active_posts_this_week.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The revised SHRINCS scheme simplifies cryptographic key management and device initialization.
- A single static master seed replaces multiple secret seeds, enhancing security and user convenience.
- The scheme's core algorithms include MasterSeedGen, KeyGen, and Sign, supporting robust security operations.

active_posts_this_week.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email discusses Bitcoin's security risks, including miner consolidation and network control issues.
- Potential decreases in Bitcoin subsidy could prompt the community to adopt controversial protective measures.
- The future economic impact of Bitcoin might be shaped by less speculative influences and potential centralization.

active_posts_this_week.title: Emulating OP_RAND
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The paper discusses enhancing blockchain security using taproot and novel funding methods.
- It introduces confidentiality methods integrating MuSig2, ZKPs, and adaptor signatures.
- Advanced cryptographic layers prevent premature access to secret transaction values.

active_posts_this_week.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
active_posts_this_week.summary: - An error in a prior proposal was identified and the idea was withdrawn.  - Acknowledgment and thanks were expressed following another party's corrective challenge.  - The incident highlights the value of peer review in problem-solving and intellectual tasks.

active_posts_this_week.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The video outlines recent innovations in software development and their applications.
- Case studies show the real-world impact of these technological advancements.
- It also discusses overcoming challenges in adopting new technologies.

active_posts_this_week.title: Proof-of-work based signet faucet
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The new mining software update supports stratumv1 ASIC miners on a test network.
- Miners can connect using the address on inquisition.bitcoin-signet.net at port 3333.
- Participants will solve blocks regularly and receive payouts within a few days.

active_posts_this_week.title: Quantum Sunset Economics: a working paper analyzing PACT adoption
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Section 3.8 highlights underestimated quantum threats from xpub data exposure.
- Institutions vulnerable to xpub leaks should consider centralized pooling services.
- The xpub mechanism's design, balancing convenience and security, is critically examined.

active_posts_this_week.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The mechanism avoids free relay in network transactions, enhancing security.
- It allows replacement cycling within the refund path, unlike `OP_EXPIRE`.
- This approach guarantees refunds if conditions are not met, reducing attack incentives.
